GridMap.description: Map extents in the header frame.
GridMap.raw:
std_msgs/Header header

# Map extents in the header frame.
geometry_msgs/Point top_left
geometry_msgs/Point top_right
geometry_msgs/Point bottom_right
geometry_msgs/Point bottom_left

# Map data layers
string[]            map_names
sensor_msgs/Image[] map_data

LeadVehicle.raw:
std_msgs/Header header

float32 headway_distance
float32 speed
float32 heading
float32 x_pos
float32 y_pos
int8 classification
int8 type

Obstacle.description: Unique id for the obstacle or empty if not used.
Obstacle.raw:
string id
# Unique id for the obstacle or empty if not used.

geometry_msgs/Pose pose
# The pose of the origin of the obstacle.

geometry_msgs/Point[] polygon
# A list of points that define the obstacle's geometry in horizontal
# plane relative to the obstacle's pose.  The polygon is implicitly
# closed by a segment between the last and first points.

ObstacleArray.description: This message is used to communicate the position of one or more
ObstacleArray.raw:
# This message is used to communicate the position of one or more
# obstacles.

std_msgs/Header header
# The header defines the frame that the obstacles are defined in.

Obstacle[] obstacles
# List of obstacles

Path.raw:
std_msgs/Header                header
PathPoint[]                    points
bool in_reverse

PathPlanning.description: The following items are used in Intelligence, but won't be transmitted with
PathPlanning.raw:
std_msgs/Header header

int32   segment_type    # Type of path segment
float32 length          # Length of path segment
float32 start_speed     # Desired speed at start of path segment
float32 end_speed       # Desired speed at end of path segment
float32 startx          # Starting x-coordinate of the vehicle
float32 starty          # Starting y-coordinate of the vehicle
float32 endx            # Ending x-coordinate of the vehicle
float32 endy            # Ending y-coordinate of the vehicle
float32 theta0          # theta0 (constant) parameter for initial heading
float32 a1              # a1 (linear) parameter for the x-y track
float32 a2              # a2 (quadratric) parameter for the x-y track
float32 k0              # k0 (constant) parameter for the curvature
float32 c1              # c1 (linear) parameter for the curvature
float32 c2              # c2 (quadratic) parameter for the curvature
int32 behavior          # Type of behavior for the segment
int32 creep             # Whether we should be in creep mode of not (0-No, 1-Yes)
int32 acc               # Whether ACC Override is on (0-Normal ACC, 1-Override ACC)
int32 reverse           # Whether we should be in reverse or not (0-No, 1-Yes)
int32 vehicle_track     # Whether we are tracking a vehicle (0-No, 1-Yes)

# The following items are used in Intelligence, but won't be transmitted with
# the segments to C&C

bool transmitted        # Flag to indicate whether the path segment has been
                        # transmitted

bool aux_transmitted

float32 theta_end       # End heading (provided explicitly)
float32 k_end           # End curvature
int32 seg_len           # Number of route points used to calculate the segment
float32 speed_limit     # Minimum speed of route points used (comes from MDF or -1)

# Other variables
float32 max_error
float32 max_smooth
float32 max_curv
int32 possible_points
bool exit_segment

fields.name: start_speed
fields.comment: Desired speed at start of path segment

fields.name: end_speed
fields.comment: Desired speed at end of path segment

fields.name: a1
fields.comment: a1 (linear) parameter for the x-y track

fields.name: a2
fields.comment: a2 (quadratric) parameter for the x-y track

fields.name: behavior
fields.comment: Type of behavior for the segment

fields.name: creep
fields.comment: Whether we should be in creep mode of not (0-No, 1-Yes)

fields.name: acc
fields.comment: Whether ACC Override is on (0-Normal ACC, 1-Override ACC)

fields.name: reverse
fields.comment: Whether we should be in reverse or not (0-No, 1-Yes)

fields.name: vehicle_track
fields.comment: Whether we are tracking a vehicle (0-No, 1-Yes)

fields.name: transmitted
fields.comment: Flag to indicate whether the path segment has been

fields.name: seg_len
fields.comment: Number of route points used to calculate the segment

fields.name: speed_limit
fields.comment: Minimum speed of route points used (comes from MDF or -1)

PathPoint.raw:
float64 x
float64 y
float32 yaw   # In radians
float32 speed # In m/s

Route.raw:
std_msgs/Header                         header
RoutePoint[]                   route_points
marti_common_msgs/KeyValue[]   properties

RouteArray.description: list of muliple routes, for instance to support multi-lane roads
RouteArray.raw:
# list of muliple routes, for instance to support multi-lane roads

std_msgs/Header                         header

marti_nav_msgs/Route[]         routes

marti_common_msgs/KeyValue[]   properties # properties common to all routes

RouteOffset.description: Gives a position and orientation relative to a position on a route
RouteOffset.raw:
# Gives a position and orientation relative to a position on a route

std_msgs/Header header                                 # only stamp used

geometry_msgs/Pose relative_pose              # pose relative to position on route
marti_nav_msgs/RoutePosition route_position   # position on route

fields.name: relative_pose
fields.comment: pose relative to position on route

RoutePoint.raw:
geometry_msgs/Pose           pose
string                       id         # unique identifier for this point
marti_common_msgs/KeyValue[] properties

RoutePosition.raw:
# Position along route

std_msgs/Header  header   # only stamp used
string  route_id # unique ID of the corresponding route
string  id       # unique ID of nearest point
float32 distance # forward along route, in meters from point identified by id
                 # field (negative values indicate the distance is backward
                 # along the route)

fields.name: route_id
fields.comment: unique ID of the corresponding route

fields.name: distance
fields.comment: forward along route, in meters from point identified by id

RouteSpeed.description: Speed at a position along route
RouteSpeed.raw:
# Speed at a position along route

std_msgs/Header  header   # only stamp used
string  id       # unique ID of nearest point
float32 distance # forward along route, in meters from point identified by id
                 # field (negative values indicate the distance is backward
                 # along the route)
float32 speed    # desired speed at the specified position

fields.name: distance
fields.comment: forward along route, in meters from point identified by id

fields.name: speed
fields.comment: desired speed at the specified position

RouteSpeedArray.description: List of target speeds along a route.
RouteSpeedArray.raw:
# List of target speeds along a route.

std_msgs/Header header        # only stamp used

RouteSpeed[] speeds
# List of speeds along a route. The interpretation of the list is
# application dependent.  It may represent a list of unrelated speeds,
# a distance vs speed curve along a route, etc.

TrackedObject.description: A list of points that define the obstacle's geometry in horizontal
TrackedObject.raw:
uint8 VEHICLE=0
uint8 PEDESTRIAN=1
uint8 UNKNOWN=255

std_msgs/Header header  # Frame and timestamp
uint16 id      # Id

geometry_msgs/PoseWithCovariance pose      # Pose in the header frame.
geometry_msgs/TwistWithCovariance velocity # Velocity in the header frame.
geometry_msgs/Vector3 linear_acceleration  # Acceleration in the header frame.
float64[9] linear_acceleration_covariance  # Row major x, y z

geometry_msgs/Point[] polygon
# A list of points that define the obstacle's geometry in horizontal
# plane relative to the obstacle's pose.  The polygon is implicitly
# closed by a segment between the last and first points.

# Estimated oriented bounding box for object classes with rectangular shapes.
float32 length                 # Length of the object in meters
float32 length_quality         # Length quality number [0,1]
float32 width                  # Width of the object in meters
float32 width_quality          # Width quality number [0,1]

uint8 classification           # Classification
float32 classification_quality # Classification quality number [0,1]

float32 existence_probability  # Existence probability [0,1]

builtin_interfaces/Duration age_duration          # Age of the track since first detection.
builtin_interfaces/Duration prediction_duration   # Age of the prediction since the active flag was last true.

bool active                    # Active flag for if the objects is currently being detected.

fields.name: length
fields.comment: Length of the object in meters

fields.name: width
fields.comment: Width of the object in meters

fields.name: age_duration
fields.comment: Age of the track since first detection.

fields.name: prediction_duration
fields.comment: Age of the prediction since the active flag was last true.

fields.name: active
fields.comment: Active flag for if the objects is currently being detected.

TrackedObjectArray.description: This message is used to communicate tracking data for one or more objects.
TrackedObjectArray.raw:
# This message is used to communicate tracking data for one or more objects.

std_msgs/Header header            # The frame that the objects are defined in.

TrackedObject[] objects  # The tracked objects.

fields.name: header
fields.comment: The frame that the objects are defined in.

VehicleControl.raw:
std_msgs/Header 		header

int32 		engine
int32 		gear
float64 	steering
float64 	throttle
float64 	brake
int16 		steering_position
int16 		gb_position

Wgs84Sample.description: This message contains an absolute position measurement (in
Wgs84Sample.raw:
# This message contains an absolute position measurement (in
# spherical/WGS-84 coordinates) and the corresponding sensor
# (e.g. antenna) location in a vehicle's relative odometry coordinate
# frame.

std_msgs/Header header
# The header contains the /tf name of the relative odometry frame and
# the timestamp of the measurement.

geometry_msgs/Point odom
# The position of the sample in the relative odometry frame.

geometry_msgs/Point wgs84
# The WGS84 measurement (x = longitude in decimal degrees, y =
# latitude in decimal degrees, z = altitude over the reference
# ellipsoid in meters)

float64[9] wgs84_covariance
# An estimate of WGS-84 measurement's covariance.

string guid # GUID of the route, as 32 hex digits
---
bool success # indicate successful run of triggered service
string message # informational, e. g. for error messages

response_fields.name: success

response_fields.name: message
response_fields.comment: informational, e. g. for error messages

GetRoute.raw:

GetRouteList.raw:

---
marti_nav_msgs/Route[] routes

response_fields.name: success
response_fields.comment: indicate successful run of triggered service

PlanRoute.raw:
std_msgs/Header header         # the frame the request and response are
                               # referenced in

geometry_msgs/Pose[] waypoints # series of points the route should pass through
bool plan_from_vehicle         # add the vehicle position as the first waypoint

---

Route  route     # planned route

bool success     # indicate successful run of service
string message   # informational, e.g. for error messages
float64 cost     # informational, cost of planned route

request_fields.name: header
request_fields.comment: the frame the request and response are

request_fields.name: waypoints
request_fields.comment: series of points the route should pass through

request_fields.name: plan_from_vehicle
request_fields.comment: add the vehicle position as the first waypoint

SaveRecordedRoute.raw:
string name      # Name for the route
string thumbnail # base64-encoded in caller defined format

---

bool success     # indicate successful run of triggered service
string message   # informational, e.g. for error messages

response_fields.name: success
response_fields.comment: indicate successful run of triggered service

SaveRoute.raw:
string name # Name to save the route as
string guid # GUID of the route, as 32 hex digits
marti_nav_msgs/Route route # The route that should be saved
string thumbnail # base64-encoded JPG or PNG thumbnail image
---
bool success # indicate successful run of triggered service
string message # informational, e. g. for error messages

request_fields.name: name
request_fields.comment: Name to save the route as

request_fields.name: guid
request_fields.comment: GUID of the route, as 32 hex digits

request_fields.name: route
request_fields.comment: The route that should be saved

request_fields.name: thumbnail
request_fields.comment: base64-encoded JPG or PNG thumbnail image

response_fields.name: success
response_fields.comment: indicate successful run of triggered service

response_fields.name: message
response_fields.comment: informational, e. g. for error messages

SetRoute.raw:
# Set a route as active

string guid # GUID of route to make active, as 32 hex digits
bool repeat # If true, run the route repeatedly

---

bool success     # indicate successful run of triggered service
string message   # informational, e.g. for error messages

request_fields.name: guid
request_fields.comment: GUID of route to make active, as 32 hex digits

request_fields.name: repeat
request_fields.comment: If true, run the route repeatedly

response_fields.name: success
response_fields.comment: indicate successful run of triggered service

UpdateRouteMetadata.description: This service is intended for modifying the properties on an existing route
UpdateRouteMetadata.raw:
# This service is intended for modifying the properties on an existing route 
# and its points.
# 
# The use of this service is a little non-intuitive.  Set route_guid to the GUID
# of the route you want to modify, and fill metadata_points with all of the points
# you want to modify.  The coordinates of the points actually don't matter; the
# "guid" property will be matched against an existing point in the route, and that
# point's properties will be replaced with the one in the new point.
string route_guid # The GUID of the route to modify
marti_nav_msgs/RoutePoint[] metadata_points # All of the points that should be modified
---
bool success
string message

request_fields.name: route_guid
request_fields.comment: The GUID of the route to modify

request_fields.name: metadata_points
request_fields.comment: All of the points that should be modified
